title: Nonlinear Least Squares Estimation for Emax Regression Models
text: can install development version emaxnls GitHub :

title: Nonlinear Least Squares Estimation for Emax Regression Models
code: library(tibble) library(emaxnls) set.seed(123)  emax_df #> # A tibble: 400 × 10 #>     dose exp_1 exp_2 rsp_1 rsp_2 cnt_a cnt_b cnt_c bin_d bin_e #>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #>  1     0     0     0  6.49     0  3.06  6.50 5.93      0     1 #>  2     0     0     0  7.81     0  5.72  3.84 5.60      0     1 #>  3     0     0     0  7.26     0  4.31  3.68 8.16      0     0 #>  4     0     0     0  7.45     0  4.03  2.86 9.38      1     1 #>  5     0     0     0  6.33     0  2.46  3.36 8.29      0     1 #>  6     0     0     0  7.13     0  4.87  8.90 7.06      0     0 #>  7     0     0     0  6.07     0  2.87  4.85 0.989     0     1 #>  8     0     0     0  5.47     1  1.07  5.34 3.34      1     1 #>  9     0     0     0  7.12     0  3.94  5.68 4.01      1     1 #> 10     0     0     0  8.21     1  7.46  8.16 6.92      1     1 #> # ℹ 390 more rows  emax_nls(   structural_model = rsp_1 ~ exp_1,    covariate_model = list(E0 ~ cnt_a, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),    data = emax_df ) #> Structural model: #>  #>   Exposure:  exp_1  #>   Response:  rsp_1  #>   Emax type: hyperbolic  #>  #> Covariate model: #>  #>   E0:       E0 ~ cnt_a  #>   Emax:     Emax ~ 1  #>   logEC50:  logEC50 ~ 1  #>  #> Coefficient table: #>  #>   label             estimate std_error t_statistic   p_value ci_lower ci_upper #> 1 E0_Intercept         4.99     0.0740        67.5 3.21e-219    4.85     5.14  #> 2 E0_cnt_a             0.498    0.0113        44.2 4.30e-155    0.476    0.520 #> 3 Emax_Intercept      10.0      0.104         96.3 7.23e-277    9.80    10.2   #> 4 logEC50_Intercept    8.27     0.0366       226.  0            8.19     8.34

title: Nonlinear Least Squares Estimation for Emax Regression Models
code: base_model <- emax_nls(   structural_model = rsp_1 ~ exp_1,    covariate_model = list(E0 ~ 1, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),    data = emax_df )  covariate_list <- list(   E0 = c("cnt_a", "cnt_b", "cnt_c", "bin_d", "bin_e"),   Emax = c("cnt_a", "cnt_b", "cnt_c", "bin_d", "bin_e") )  final_mod <- base_model |>    emax_scm_forward(candidates = covariate_list, threshold = .01) |>    emax_scm_backward(candidates = covariate_list, threshold = .001)   emax_scm_history(final_mod) #> # A tibble: 32 × 11 #>    iteration attempt step       action term_tested  model_tested model_converged #>        <int>   <int> <chr>      <chr>  <chr>        <chr>        <lgl>           #>  1         0       0 base model <NA>   <NA>         E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  2         1       1 forward    add    E0 ~ cnt_c   E0 ~ 1 + cn… TRUE            #>  3         1       2 forward    add    Emax ~ bin_e E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  4         1       3 forward    add    E0 ~ cnt_b   E0 ~ 1 + cn… TRUE            #>  5         1       4 forward    add    Emax ~ cnt_c E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  6         1       5 forward    add    Emax ~ cnt_a E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  7         1       6 forward    add    Emax ~ bin_d E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  8         1       7 forward    add    E0 ~ cnt_a   E0 ~ 1 + cn… TRUE            #>  9         1       8 forward    add    Emax ~ cnt_b E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #> 10         1       9 forward    add    E0 ~ bin_e   E0 ~ 1 + bi… TRUE            #> # ℹ 22 more rows #> # ℹ 4 more variables: term_p_value <dbl>, model_aic <dbl>, model_bic <dbl>, #> #   model_updated <lgl>  final_mod #> Structural model: #>  #>   Exposure:  exp_1  #>   Response:  rsp_1  #>   Emax type: hyperbolic  #>  #> Covariate model: #>  #>   E0:       E0 ~ 1 + cnt_a  #>   Emax:     Emax ~ 1  #>   logEC50:  logEC50 ~ 1  #>  #> Coefficient table: #>  #>   label             estimate std_error t_statistic   p_value ci_lower ci_upper #> 1 E0_Intercept         4.99     0.0740        67.5 3.21e-219    4.85     5.14  #> 2 E0_cnt_a             0.498    0.0113        44.2 4.30e-155    0.476    0.520 #> 3 Emax_Intercept      10.0      0.104         96.3 7.23e-277    9.80    10.2   #> 4 logEC50_Intercept    8.27     0.0366       226.  0            8.19     8.34

text: data frame columns: id Identifier column dose Nominal dose, units specified exp_1 Exposure value, units metric specified exp_2 Exposure value, units metric specified, different exp_1 rsp_1 Continuous response value (units specified) rsp_2 Binary response value (group labels specified) cnt_a Continuous valued covariate cnt_b Continuous valued covariate cnt_c Continuous valued covariate bin_d Binary valued covariate bin_e Binary valued covariate cat_f Categorical covariate

title: Sample simulated data for Emax exposure-response models with covariates. — emax_df
text: simulated dataset entirely synthetic. generic data set can used illustrate Emax modeling. contains variables corresponding dose exposure, includes continuous response variable binary response variable. Three continuous valued covariates included, along two binary covariates. can find data generating code package source code, R/data.R

title: Sample simulated data for Emax exposure-response models with covariates. — emax_df

title: Construct Emax prediction function from model object — emax_fun
Construct Emax prediction function from model object — emax_fun
Construct Emax prediction function from model object — emax_fun
text: function f arguments data params. data argument defaults data used estimate model, params arugment defaults estimated parameter values. can customized, long data contains columns corresponding variables used model, params named numeric vector appropriate length. names params must exactly match names vector returned coef(mod). return value f numeric vector model predictions row data, evaluated parameters params.

title: Construct Emax prediction function from model object — emax_fun
code: mod <- emax_nls(   structural_model = rsp_1 ~ exp_1,    covariate_model = list(E0 ~ cnt_a, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),    data = emax_df ) par <- coef(mod)  # customizable emax function with the same structural  # model and same covariate model, defaulting to the  # same data and parameters as the original model, but # allowing user to pass their own data and parameters   mod_fn <- emax_fun(mod)  # apply the function to a few rows of the original data mod_fn(   data = emax_df[120:125, ],   param = par ) #> [1] 14.650737  8.467557 13.263190 15.728956  6.255590 13.622004  # adjust the parameters new_par <- par new_par["E0_Intercept"] <- 0  # simulate the model with the adjusted parameters mod_fn(   data = emax_df[120:125, ],   param = new_par ) #> [1]  9.595929  3.412750  8.208382 10.674149  1.200782  8.567196

title: Construct an initial guess for the Emax model parameters — emax_nls_init
Construct an initial guess for the Emax model parameters — emax_nls_init
code: # use a heuristic to construct sensible start values, and plausible # upper and lower bounds within which the estimate is expected to fall  emax_nls_init(   structural_model = rsp_1 ~ exp_1,    covariate_model = list(E0 ~ cnt_a, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),    data = emax_df ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 5 #>   parameter covariate start  lower upper #>   <chr>     <chr>     <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 E0        Intercept  9.73  0.528  18.9 #> 2 Emax      Intercept  7.74 -1.47   16.9 #> 3 logEC50   Intercept  8.69  6.63   10.8  # compare to the values estimated: coef(emax_nls(   structural_model = rsp_1 ~ exp_1,    covariate_model = list(E0 ~ cnt_a, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),    data = emax_df )) #>          E0_cnt_a      E0_Intercept    Emax_Intercept logEC50_Intercept  #>         0.4861467         5.0548075         9.9697250         8.2688405

what: Settings used to estimate Emax model — emax_nls_options
title: Settings used to estimate Emax model — emax_nls_options
Settings used to estimate Emax model — emax_nls_options
code: emax_nls_options(   optim_method = "port",   optim_control = NULL,   quiet = FALSE,   weights = NULL,   na.action = options("na.action") )

title: Settings used to estimate Emax model — emax_nls_options
text: optim_method Character string specifying algorithm used solve nonlinear least squares optimization problem. Supported pptions "gauss", "port", "levenberg". See details. optim_control list arguments used control behavior optimization algorithm. Allowed values differ depending algorithm used quiet quiet=TRUE, messages suppressed weights Numeric vector providing weights observations. specified, weighted least squares used na.action missing values data handled present three supported values optim_method: "gauss": Estimate parameters using Gauss-Newton algorithm. equivalent using "default" option nls() "port": Estimate parameters using bounded optimization "nl2sol" algorithm Port library. Equivalent "port" nls() "levenberg": Estimate parameters using Levenberg-Marquardt algorithm. equivalent using nlsLM() "minpack.lm" package. Note Golub-Pereyra algorithm partially linear least-squares (.e. "plinear" option nls()) currently supported Emax regression. Informal testing suggests perform well models, rarely converges.

title: Settings used to estimate Emax model — emax_nls_options

title: Stepwise covariate modelling for Emax regression — emax_scm
Stepwise covariate modelling for Emax regression — emax_scm
code: emax_scm_forward(mod, candidates, threshold = 0.01, seed = NULL)  emax_scm_backward(mod, candidates, threshold = 0.001, seed = NULL)  emax_scm_history(mod)

title: Stepwise covariate modelling for Emax regression — emax_scm
text: mod emaxnls object candidates list candidate covariates threshold Threshold addition removal seed Seed RNG state

title: Stepwise covariate modelling for Emax regression — emax_scm
Stepwise covariate modelling for Emax regression — emax_scm
code: base_model <- emax_nls(rsp_1 ~ exp_1, list(E0 ~ 1, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1), emax_df)  covariate_list <- list(   E0 = c("cnt_a", "cnt_b", "cnt_c", "bin_d", "bin_e"),   Emax = c("cnt_a", "cnt_b", "cnt_c", "bin_d", "bin_e") )  mm <- emax_scm_forward(   mod = base_model,   candidates = covariate_list,    threshold = .01 ) final_mod <- emax_scm_backward(   mod = mm,   candidates = covariate_list,    threshold = .001 )   final_mod #> Structural model: #>  #>   Exposure:  exp_1  #>   Response:  rsp_1  #>   Emax type: hyperbolic  #>  #> Covariate model: #>  #>   E0:       E0 ~ 1 + cnt_a  #>   Emax:     Emax ~ 1  #>   logEC50:  logEC50 ~ 1  #>  #> Coefficient table: #>  #>   label             estimate std_error t_statistic   p_value ci_lower ci_upper #> 1 E0_cnt_a             0.486    0.0116        42.1 3.63e-148    0.463    0.509 #> 2 E0_Intercept         5.05     0.0759        66.6 4.16e-217    4.91     5.20  #> 3 Emax_Intercept       9.97     0.112         89.3 2.11e-264    9.75    10.2   #> 4 logEC50_Intercept    8.27     0.0394       210.  0            8.19     8.35   emax_scm_history(final_mod) #> # A tibble: 22 × 11 #>    iteration attempt step       action term_tested  model_tested model_converged #>        <int>   <int> <chr>      <chr>  <chr>        <chr>        <lgl>           #>  1         0       0 base model NA     NA           E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  2         1       1 forward    add    Emax ~ cnt_b E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  3         1       2 forward    add    E0 ~ bin_e   E0 ~ 1 + bi… TRUE            #>  4         1       3 forward    add    Emax ~ cnt_a E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  5         1       4 forward    add    Emax ~ bin_e E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  6         1       5 forward    add    E0 ~ bin_d   E0 ~ 1 + bi… TRUE            #>  7         1       6 forward    add    E0 ~ cnt_a   E0 ~ 1 + cn… TRUE            #>  8         1       7 forward    add    Emax ~ bin_d E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  9         1       8 forward    add    Emax ~ cnt_c E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #> 10         1       9 forward    add    E0 ~ cnt_c   E0 ~ 1 + cn… TRUE            #> # ℹ 12 more rows #> # ℹ 4 more variables: term_p_value <dbl>, model_aic <dbl>, model_bic <dbl>, #> #   model_updated <lgl>

title: Add or remove a covariate term from an Emax regression — emax_update
Add or remove a covariate term from an Emax regression — emax_update
code: mod_0 <- emax_nls(rsp_1 ~ exp_1, list(E0 ~ 1, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1), emax_df) mod_1 <- emax_nls(rsp_1 ~ exp_1, list(E0 ~ cnt_a, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1), emax_df)  emax_add_term(mod_0, E0 ~ cnt_a) #> Structural model: #>  #>   Exposure:  exp_1  #>   Response:  rsp_1  #>   Emax type: hyperbolic  #>  #> Covariate model: #>  #>   E0:       E0 ~ 1 + cnt_a  #>   Emax:     Emax ~ 1  #>   logEC50:  logEC50 ~ 1  #>  #> Coefficient table: #>  #>   label             estimate std_error t_statistic   p_value ci_lower ci_upper #> 1 E0_cnt_a             0.486    0.0116        42.1 3.63e-148    0.463    0.509 #> 2 E0_Intercept         5.05     0.0759        66.6 4.16e-217    4.91     5.20  #> 3 Emax_Intercept       9.97     0.112         89.3 2.11e-264    9.75    10.2   #> 4 logEC50_Intercept    8.27     0.0394       210.  0            8.19     8.35  emax_remove_term(mod_1, E0 ~ cnt_a) #> Structural model: #>  #>   Exposure:  exp_1  #>   Response:  rsp_1  #>   Emax type: hyperbolic  #>  #> Covariate model: #>  #>   E0:       E0 ~ 1  #>   Emax:     Emax ~ 1  #>   logEC50:  logEC50 ~ 1  #>  #> Coefficient table: #>  #>   label             estimate std_error t_statistic   p_value ci_lower ci_upper #> 1 E0_Intercept          7.42    0.119         62.4 2.52e-207     7.19     7.66 #> 2 Emax_Intercept        9.86    0.251         39.3 7.31e-139     9.37    10.4  #> 3 logEC50_Intercept     8.16    0.0931        87.6 7.61e-262     7.97     8.35

title: Predicting from Emax regression models — predict.emaxnls
text: object emaxnls object newdata named list data frame look variables predict. newdata missing fitted values original data points returned. se.fit switch indicating standard errors required. interval character string indicating prediction intervals confidence interval mean responses calculated. Can "none", "confidence", "prediction" level numeric scalar 0 1 giving confidence level intervals () calculated. ... Ignored

title: Predicting from Emax regression models — predict.emaxnls
text: return value differs slightly depending inputs. se.fit = FALSE, produces vector matrix predictions column names fit, lwr upr interval argument set. se.fit = TRUE, returns list following components: fit: vector matrix se.fit: standard error predicted means residual.scale: residual standard deviation df: residual degrees freedom
